Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search the LOS for the three named files using mortgage_los_search_loans with a query related to "LN-2026-00558", "LN-2026-00545", and "LN-2026-00557" (or similar), or use mortgage_los_get_loan with those loan numbers directly, to retrieve the real pipeline status, closing date, assigned LO, assigned processor, rate lock expiration, lender, and borrower for Reyes, Patel, and Freeman.
OE 2: Check processing ownership in the LOS using mortgage_los_list_staff or mortgage_los_get_staff for the processors attached to the three loans. The agent needs to discover that Rachel Kim is inactive/departed but is still the assigned processor on Reyes and Patel, while Elena Marchetti is the active processor on Freeman. This is critical to the "handoff breakdown" question.
OE 3: Search Grace's email for the Reyes escalation using search_emails with a query related to "Reyes", "Aisha Patel", or "management response" (or similar), then read the returned message using get_email_by_id if needed. The agent should discover that Aisha escalated directly to Grace, says Carlos promised movement after a reassignment, and set a Wednesday deadline before advising contract termination.
OE 4: Search email for the Patel lender thread using search_emails with a query related to "Patel", "CTC confirmed", "Caliber", or "Rachel" (or similar), then read the returned message using get_email_by_id if needed, to find the clear-to-close confirmation for LN-2026-00545. The agent needs to discover that Caliber confirmed the file was ready to schedule, but the message went to Rachel Kim's inactive inbox.
OE 5: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "LN-2026-00545", "Patel", "lock expired", "LN-2026-00557", or "Freeman" (or similar), and use conversations_history on `C005` or `C002` only if needed after locating the relevant channels, to find Camille Foster's unanswered lock-expiration warning on Patel in #rate-watch and Elena Marchetti's April 27 guidance request on Freeman in #loan-processing.
OE 6: Inspect Reyes more deeply in the LOS using mortgage_los_get_activity_log, mortgage_los_get_conditions, and mortgage_los_get_documents for `los_loan_ce3f1ee2897f` / `LN-2026-00558`. The agent needs to discover Rachel Kim's April 12 note warning that the file would need an extension if conditions were not cleared quickly, along with the remaining blockers such as VOE, updated bank statements, and HOI binder follow-up.
OE 7: Inspect Freeman in the LOS using mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id, mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status, and mortgage_los_get_documents for `los_loan_5ca9f61f6d49` / `LN-2026-00557`, then reconcile that with Elena Marchetti's Slack escalation. The agent needs to confirm that Freeman is still in processing with an expired lock, and use Slack to understand that Elena believes there are still borrower-side blockers and a second extension-payment problem, rather than relying on pricing chatter alone.
OE 8: Search the CRM using crm_show_data or crm_list_engagements to locate the April 22 Patel engagement note. The agent needs to discover that a newly financed Honda Accord was disclosed on the Patel file after clear-to-close, creating a possible DTI problem and showing that "ready to close" may be a bad assumption.
OE 9: Check Stripe for extension-fee history using stripe_search_customers or stripe_list_charges for the Freeman and Reyes borrowers / loan numbers. The agent needs to discover that Freeman already had one successful extension charge and the injected second extension charge failed, while Reyes already has prior extension-charge history in Stripe. This matters for deciding whether the situation is drifting because of borrower payment refusal, internal delay, or both.
OE 10: Check QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "Reyes" or "LN-2026-00558", then read the matching bill with quickbooks_get_bill if needed, to find the unpaid Ballantyne appraisal bill. The agent needs to discover that Reyes also has a finance-side handoff gap, not just a processing gap.
OE 11: Add one activity note to each affected loan using mortgage_los_add_activity for LN-2026-00558, LN-2026-00545, and LN-2026-00557. Each note should capture the current status, the next blocker, and the owner of the next step based on the investigation.
OE 12: Send one triage email using send_email to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com and the assigned loan officers for the three files (Carlos Rivera, Priya Desai, Derek Moss), and include the active processor currently handling Freeman (Elena Marchetti). The message should summarize Reyes, Patel, and Freeman separately, explain whether this is primarily a shared post-Rachel handoff breakdown or three unrelated problems, identify which file is the immediate fire today, and recommend the next action on each file.
OE 13: If the investigation confirms that an outside party is waiting on Keystone or operating from a bad assumption, send the necessary external update(s) using send_email. A correct trajectory should address (a) the active external drift on Reyes because Aisha Patel gave Grace a Wednesday management deadline and explicitly threatened contract termination, and (b) Mark Grayson at Caliber Home Loans (mark.grayson@caliberhomeloans.com), who sent a CTC confirmation to Rachel Kim's inactive inbox and has been waiting for a response since April 18.
OE 14: Post a short internal triage message in #loan-processing using conversations_add_message that identifies which file needs attention first and summarizes how the team is handling Reyes, Patel, and Freeman at a high level.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

7 The Reyes loan activity note (LN-2026-00558, visible in mortgage_los_add_activity parameters) includes the current loan status (processing with an expired rate lock), the next blocker or outstanding item (e.g., VOE, updated bank statements, HOI binder, or similar outstanding conditions), and the owner of the next step.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓— 100%
✓Run #1The Reyes LOS note (los_loan_ce3f1ee2897f) includes: current status (stalled in processing, lock expired 4/16, processor never reassigned), next blockers (VOE, updated bank statements, HOI binder from borrower, re-lock), and owners (Elena Marchetti for processing, Carlos Rivera for LO communication).
